explanations.8: nohup runs the chrome command in the background so the script doesn't wait for it to finish, and the backslash lets us continue the command on the next line.
explanations.10: The > /dev/null 2>&1 & redirects all output to nowhere and the & at the end puts chrome in the background so we can continue with the script.
explanations.13: $! is a special variable that holds the process ID (PID) of the last command we ran in the background, so we can shut it down later.
explanations.15: This tells the shell checker to ignore warnings about unquoted variables on line 16, which is safe here because behat handles spaces in filenames correctly.
explanations.19: We're using braces like ${chromePID} instead of $chromePID to clearly show where the variable name ends, especially useful if there were text right after it.
